raw_input:
[4.0/5] Awesome, but for AA hair you may need to shave again with a regular razor Awesome, but for AA hair you may need to shave again with a regular razor. Especially in the nape area. It is best used with a mirror or someone who can check behind to see if anything is missed. [2.0/5] Update: suddenly died. Keep your friends close, keep your shaves closer [[VIDEOID:26b251054fc6c6ec321edf330079ffc6]] This thing shaved my head as close as a razor blade does. It is pretty much the closest shave you can get with an electric shaver.

I have had 5 and 6 head shavers but this one goes to seven! And that many rotating heads make your shave go quicker.

It fits in my hand and is comfortable to hold. It's USB-C rechargeable which is super convenient.

Update: it had a glitch and stopped working, I took some tweezers and rotated the gear inside the main unit manually and it started working again.

Update: it suddenly stopped working again and this time it will not start. I really liked this shaver but it just abruptly died. It's charged, and the lights and display work but the motor doea not run. I can not use this. [3.0/5] Absolutely adequate Does a fine job, works wet, just don’t use on your face. I’m left with some half gnarly razor burn all over rn lol. But I bought it for primary use to stay near bald and in that it did alright so for the price I’ll keep it [4.0/5] 7 head shaver. Waterproof. The on/off button is easy to accidentally toggle when using First off, I'm going to review this for face/beard shaving. I don't have a bald head (yet, anyway), so that isn't my focus.

I have used rotary floating blade shavers for decades (usually Norelco). They work best on my face, especially for the neck region. These shavers have always featured three heads.

Well, here we have seven heads. Seven heads!! I have used very similar shavers to this one with five and six-head shaver attachments. This is one more. The question is: Why do the high-quality venerable brands stick to three heads, and these shavers use so many more?

The answer isn't quality of shave. In fact, I was impressed at how well this shaver cleared the stubble on my face at the end of the day. It was comfortable, and managed to navigate the nooks and crannies of my face and neck as well as my low-end Norelco (if not better). But it's no match--not even remotely close--to my top-of-the-line Norelco (though that costs about 4x as much, so that's probably not a fair comparison). But--and this is huge--the ergonomics of the shaving heads leave something to desire. First off, there's very little reservoir under the cutting heads for shaved hair (see photos). This means you'll need/want to wash the head (it's water immersible) after each shave. Second, the middle head of the seven doesn't pop up. To remove it, you have to twist it off. It's a bit of a pain--and there's even less room under this head for hair than any of the others.

The third issue I have with the cutting heads is that there's no obvious way to replace them without simply replacing the entire shaving head assembly. Based on my experience with other shavers quite similar to this, expect the blades to get dull after a bit of use, and factor in the cost of an entire replacement head unit when they do.

In terms of seven vs six vs five heads? Well, the motors in these shavers are effectively the same. So if you have seven heads, the amount of power you provide to each head is less than it is in a five head design. That's going to affect the maximum speed and also the ability to easily tackle heavy stubble. Given that I don't sense a huge amount of improvement with seven vs five (or six) heads, I'd be inclined to veer towards fewer heads.

In general, I'd suggest that you can interchange the 7/6/5 head assemblies if you want to get a replacement.

In the kit you get:

Seven head cordless shaver with LCD readout of charge remaining
Waterproof (IPX level not indicated)
Nose hair trimmer attachment
Hair clipper attachment
Two face cleaning attachments
Three length guides for the hair clipper attachment
Charging cable (requires USB-A power source). The charge port on the shaver is a standard USB-C port.
Small cleaning brush

Shave is controlled via a simple on/off button on the back. Given how you would normally hold this--palmed (see photo)--I found it annoyingly easy to accidentally push the power button when I was using it.

The non-shaver attachments are a mixed bag. On the plus side, the nose hair trimmer works very well. This is in contrast with the nose hair trimmers that come with clippers, which are worthless. The nose hair clipper here works as well as the dedicated nose hair clippers I've used. On the minus side, the hair clipper is quite mediocre--it operates at a low speed and struggles with any amount of hair. It's about as good/useful as the clipper that's integrated into some shaver handles. Those can clip a bit of trim hair, but they are an exercise in frustration for any serious hair removal. Same here. The face cleaner attachments are probably OK, but I can't imagine using them.

On the whole, I'd consider this a decent little kit useful for shaving your face and for nose hair removal. The remaining attachments are, in my opinion, not very useful. While it does a good job of shaving out of the box, my experience is that the heads get dull somewhat quickly, and you need to buy a not-inexpensive replacement assembly. Also, cleaning the assembly is a nuisance.

Pros:
** 7 head shaver does a good job on face
** Waterproof (IPX level not indicated)
** Nose hair clipper works well
** Li-Ion battery: 2 hours charge for 90 minutes use
** Charge port on shaver is standard USB-C, so you can use charge cables you have at home
** Can lock the power button for travel (by holding for 3 seconds)

Cons:
** Too easy to push the power button when palming the shaver in the hand during use
** Hair trimmer has limited usefulness
** Face cleaning attachments not very significant
** Shaver head assembly doesn't provide much room for hair and will need to be cleaned after each shave
** Middle head of shaver attachment difficult to remove for cleaning
** Must replace entire shaving head unit when one/more blades get dull
** 7th head in shaver assembly does not float like outer heads

Note that there is a dizzying number of similar 5/6/7 head shavers sold on Amazon. As far as I can tell, having reviewed many of them, they are all made by a handful of companies and work pretty much equivalently well. The snap-in attachments even work across different models and brands. There are variances in features like level of waterproof and how the power switch works (and the body shape), but the function is pretty similar. So shop around for price. [5.0/5] Love it Love it its excellent [5.0/5] Straight to the skin I like it it goes straight to the skin no Razor bumps [4.0/5] As described I've used it a couple of times and it does what it's supposed to, the minutes remaining indicator comes in handy, easy to clean and it's nice to have the attachments. Have only used it to dry shave my head, trying wet next [3.0/5] does the job well Hard to clean, battery does not last very long, But it does do a good job on scalp. [5.0/5] great shaver 7 heads shaves my head clean and fast [5.0/5] You can use it with shaving cream and clean the blade section with water. Gives me a quality close shave on my face and head and is very easy to clean. [1.0/5] Cheap and unreliable. I purchased the 7d head shaver and after about 2 months of semi-regular use and cleaning as specified by the manufacturer, the device can hardly get a grip on my hair to shave and has developed an incredibly irritating high pitched whine/screech to it. It was loud and annoying when I first turned it on but I could deal with it as the cost of having an electric shaver, I never expected it to be silent. This new shrieking noise is unacceptable. It always took multiple passes to get a decent clean shave on my head, but now it takes an exponentially greater amount of passes with the shaver to get anywhere close to a shave with this device. The accessories, when used with a light touch as described in the manual, have also developed structural issues and are also unusable. One can expect this device to last about 2 months before becoming garbage.

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, customers have mixed opinions about this 7-head electric shaver. On the positive side, many users find it gives a close, clean shave on both the head and face, works well on bald heads, is easy to clean, and features convenient USB-C charging. Several customers appreciate the waterproofing, the nose hair trimmer attachment, and the LCD battery indicator.

However, there are notable drawbacks. Multiple reviewers experienced reliability issues, with their units suddenly stopping working after a short period of use. One customer reported the device developed a loud, irritating high-pitched noise after just two months and became essentially unusable. The power button is easy to accidentally press while holding the shaver, and the hair clipper attachment is considered weak and not very useful. Cleaning the shaving head assembly can be a nuisance, particularly the middle head. Some users also note that the blades may dull relatively quickly, requiring replacement of the entire head assembly.

The shaver is generally seen as a decent budget option for head shaving, performing adequately for that purpose, though it may not match the quality of premium brands like Norelco. The consensus is that it works well enough for the price, but long-term durability is a concern.
